testcase.name: 1030-rule-node-typed-dimension-rule-testcase
Rule Node Typed Dimension Rule Testcase
testcase.description: Table linkbase test case using a rule node with a typed dimension rule

variation.name: Simple Table, rows: primary items, columns: typed dimension
variation.description: Simple table with two columns, and two rows (primary items m1, m2).

variation.name: v-01 with instance data
variation.description: Same as v-01 with instance data.

variation.name: Rule nodes with typed dimension rules that use XPath expressions
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items Number of Employees, Average Salary) and three columns (typed dimension Age = ..-20, 21-40, 41-..). The typed values are specified by XPath expressions that extract them from the instance. In this variation there is no instance, so the table cannot be rendered.

variation.name: Rule nodes with typed dimension rules that use XPath expressions, with instance
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items Number of Employees, Average Salary) and three columns (typed dimension Age = ..-20, 21-40, 41-..). The typed values are specified by XPath expressions that extract them from the instance, but this will not work as the context item for these expressions is undefined.

variation.name: Rule node with typed dimension rule that has omit
variation.description: Simple table with a two rows (primary items Maximum Salary, Bonus) and six columns (typed dimension Age = ..-20, 21-40, 41-.. and typed dimension Department = Sales, Admin). Values for the Sales department are not broken down by age but apply to all ages, so the age constraint is removed for these columns. In this variation there is no instance.

variation.name: Rule node with typed dimension rule that has omit, with instance
variation.description: Simple table with a two rows (primary items Maximum Salary, Bonus) and six columns (typed dimension Age = ..-20, 21-40, 41-.. and typed dimension Department = Sales, Admin). Values for the Sales department are not broken down by age but apply to all ages, so the age constraint is removed for these columns.

variation.name: Simple Table with an incomplete typed dimension rule.
variation.description: Simple table with one x axis node for an incomplete typed dimension rule.
